artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2021-03-19
artifacts.excerpt:
When I ask questions on twitter, especially in a reply to someone I don't know well, I do my best to be genuine and earnest, rather than sarcastic/rhetorical.

Saying this out loud to normalize it, because I think twitter is better when people do this.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2026-04-08
artifacts.excerpt:
I don't say this lightly: fuck this shit.

I find this to be abhorrent.

Different people can feel differently about it, of course!

But I think it's worth saying how I feel about it, because I think this is bad for people, and being quiet just lets this get normalized. https://t.co/X86GAcafp5
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, think, want.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2025-07-02

Context: I… don’t really care much about software development any more. Doesn’t do much for me. Maybe it will again in the future, maybe if I’m doing my own thing, dunno! But it has felt for a while so disconnected from any sense of…

artifacts.title: Thanks receipt from 2021-03-31
artifacts.excerpt:
@stevenstrogatz @veritasium @SciencePetr Thanks for your contribution to this, Dr. Strogatz! I just watched this with my 9 year old son and we both enjoyed it a lot (as is usually the case with @veritasium videos; no surprise there).

Your enthusiasm for this topic comes across clearly and is inspiring :)
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: thanks, cool, awesome, sharing.

artifacts.title: Thanks receipt from 2022-05-31
artifacts.excerpt:
One of my favorite things to reply w/on twitter is "for anyone else in the replies curious as I was here's a link"

Takes a few seconds, and the thanks you get is instant warm fuzzies

It's like the perfect helpful "yes, and" to the OP
